page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg9i.24xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor. Running on the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped directly to 96 physical cores, operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz with a single thread per core configuration. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

This instance does not include local storage, requiring reliance on external network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 48 Gbps, facilitating high-throughput data transfers. The instance does not feature any GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The dedicated core allocation ensures consistent compute performance without resource contention, though the lack of hyperthreading means workloads cannot leverage virtual thread-level parallelism.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.hfg9i.24xlarge offers a high-density compute profile with dedicated physical resources, making it suitable for workloads requiring predictable CPU performance without the overhead of shared-core architectures. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this server for general-purpose enterprise applications, high-performance web serving, batch processing, and medium-scale database hosting where dedicated CPU execution is critical.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg9i.24xlarge is an x86_64 server instance featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by 3.4 GHz Intel Xeon 6982P-C processors. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it allocates one thread per physical core, ensuring dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading. The instance is configured with 384.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core ratio. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 48 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for workloads requiring predictable, high-frequency CPU performance, such as enterprise applications, batch processing, and web serving.
